mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in FEDFUNDS over the trailing 3 months signals an easing monetary policy stance that lowers the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during FEDFUNDS decline periods are not significantly different from baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month rise in UNRATE exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals a sudden deterioration in labor market conditions, triggering risk-off equity selling and negative S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.2pp month-over-month
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following UNRATE spikes exceeding the threshold are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration of RSAFS and HOUST above their respective trend growth rates indicates broad-based consumer and housing demand strength that compounds into sustained corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon; neither series alone carries this predictive power.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 3% AND HOUST 3-month annualized growth > 5%
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following joint triggers are not significantly higher than returns following single-series triggers (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion where FEDFUNDS is rising for three consecutive months while CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation exceeds 2.5%, further tightening signals restrictive financial conditions that outweigh growth momentum, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days; outside this joint inflation-tightening regime the same FEDFUNDS increase shows no reliable directional effect.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rising for 3 consecutive months AND CPIAUCSL YoY > 2.5%
regime_hint: late-cycle monetary tightening with rising inflation
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns during joint tightening-inflation triggers are not significantly more negative than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or the effect reverses sign in more than one of three consecutive rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained INDPRO growth above 2% annualized over the trailing quarter signals expanding industrial output and corporate revenue capacity, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter as earnings expectations are revised upward.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns during periods of INDPRO growth above 2% annualized do not exceed baseline returns by a statistically significant margin (p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly PAYEMS net job gain falling below 100,000 signals an unexpected deceleration in labor demand ahead of consensus expectations, prompting short-term risk-off equity flows and negative S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following sub-100k PAYEMS prints are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: GDPC1 quarterly annualized growth falling below 2% signals a slowing real economy during a mature expansion, leading investors to revise down forward earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter, particularly late in the business cycle.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following sub-2% GDPC1 prints in late-cycle periods are not significantly more negative than baseline (p > 0.05), or the pattern fails to replicate in early/mid-cycle subsamples used as placebo.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous FEDFUNDS increases and UNRATE readings below 4% reflect a tightening labor market that prompts hawkish Fed policy, jointly elevating recession risk and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days; neither a rising FEDFUNDS nor a low UNRATE alone reliably predicts this outcome.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rising 3-month trend AND UNRATE below 4%
regime_hint: monetary tightening with tight labor market
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns during joint FEDFUNDS-rising/UNRATE-below-4% triggers are not significantly more negative than returns following either single-series condition alone (p > 0.05) across a 3-year rolling evaluation.
